comment: It is difficult, he is a math nerd. Some concepts that he thinks easy maybe not easy for student, so sometimes he will miss some explain on some terms.

I took his class 25 winter. Hi doesn't take attendance. Published lecture notes and ponapto recordings. His handwriting wasn't good but understandable. He used to answer my questions at 12 at night, which is great. The exams wasn't difficult. Most are justification (easy to get scores) and calculation is not heavy.

Exams are worth a large majority of your grade so even if you get nearly/full points on both textbook and conceptual problem homework, you will probably end up barely passing if you don't do well on the 3 exams. The two midterm exams didn't give enough time to make a good attempt on each problem since you only get 50 minutes.

The conceptual HW is pretty difficult, but that's just the nature of the course. Christian is good at explaining concepts, but he often wouldn't be able to cover all the planned material. Material he didn't cover wouldn't be on the exam tho. Exams are okay in difficulty, just prepare your cheat sheet very well.

He's a new professor and will probably get better but this quarter was not good. Got behind schedule in the first week and we were barely getting to cover the content before exams even then. Definitely needs to improve organization. Lectures tend to spend way too much time explaining trivial concepts deeply.

Still a very new professor, so I take everything here with a grain of salt. Lectures were very boring, was not super good at boiling down concepts into understandable terms initially, doesn't help that the class was at 8:30 AM. Showed ability to adapt and change as quarter progressed, will probably be much better later. Tests were not too bad imo.

Second-quarter postdoctoral scholar, saw he's going into his third quarter teaching the same class. Introduces concepts somewhat well from an abstract standpoint, not much actual examples. Often ends up in circular or dead-end explanations, sometimes I left OH more puzzled. Read textbook and watch Thomas Car Math Lectures before coming to lectures.

The lectures were not very helpful for me, and I relied on Thomas Carr videos and the textbook mostly (as did many other students). The conceptual homework was difficult and long. Your grade depended heavily on the exams which were much longer and harder compared to other classes (even though our class was a week behind schedule / other sections).

He's trying his best and he's super sweet, but unfortunately his explainations didnt work for me and I just watched Thomas. Always at least one problem on the exam that you've never seen before, but other then that he's not thatttt bad. He has a good heart? Idk it was pretty rough ngl, but I can't bash on him he's so sweet.
Doesn't seem to consider student feedback. Exams are still unreasonably long and difficult while being behind other sections.

Lectures are very content heavy in terms of niche conceptual stuff but barely covers actual math. Lectures are hard to follow. Spends a lot of time on random proofs and falls behind the other classes. Tests are long and difficult with many long and niche questions. Way harder than other classes that have more math and base level concepts.
All people from last quarter says how he would change. Not really, still longest exam and hard compared to all other professors.

Poor teaching, progress is 1-2 chapters behind than other sections, while he is not good at teaching, unclear what to say. Goes really slow yet did not explain much. Also his exam will be the toughest and most questions compared to all other professor. He seems nice, but an awful professor and seems like he has never taught before and awful exam

(Subject to change - he is new) Lectures are sometimes confusing since he goes over the concepts abstractly and sometimes go in circles in some concepts for the whole lecture. His tests are much longer and a little harder than other tests I have seen. The TAs are pretty helpful tho since I have to study most of the stuff on my own.

He's nice and that's about it. Exams were much harder than past quarters, and also on a time crunch. Homework is mainly conceptual problems with a little bit of computation. Lectures not recorded, but doesn't really matter since they kinda suck. (Thomas Carr YT FTW) He is new, so subject to change, or not. Or just choose one of the 5/5 profs.

He's a new teacher and you can tell, but he's super receptive to student feedback and you can tell he wants to be a good teacher. Math is hard always, but he does a good job explaining concepts, gives a reasonable workload and grades really quickly (grading is sometimes inconsistent though).

*New professor so this will likely change.* Lectures were very difficult to follow, but he responded to student feedback within the quarter. I think he'll be a great professor eventually, but this quarter wasn't good.

disclaimer: this is his first quarter so improvement will occur. lectures are very unorganized and sort of just jumps from topic to topic. very abstract and no examples of homework problems or how to solve anything that would be on the test. midterms and final also seem significantly harder and longer than any previous exam in the 208 archive.

Lectures often got very convoluted and messy, abstract topics were often taught somewhat unclearly and frequently going off on irrelevant tangents. Lectures weren't recorded which was a bit inconveniencing but he did post both the lecture note templates and lecture notes. Midterms were a bit too long for the short amount of time given to do them.

(He's new, so this is subject to change.) - Lectures taught me pretty well, personally, but sometimes it got confusing & pointless - Not recorded :( - lectures were often very abstract - theorems instead of examples If you read the textbook it should be fine

Professor seemed pretty new. I think the lectures were a bit confusing, but linear algebra seems to have multiple notations and definitions that can get convoluted. Read the textbook and its practice problems. Lectures were often similar to the textbook, so if I read along during lectures, I could watch and look at the textbook when I got confused.

Don't really get the bad comments, Yes, lectures weren't recorded but that's due to the lecture hall having no recording equipment. Lectures are ok, he provides good lecture notes and does a somewhat decent job at explaining concepts. accessible outside class, always ready to help. Lots of homework and study outside class needed though.

Professor doesn't do the bare minimum the school requires, doesn't record lecture just because he doesn't feel like it. The TA's are easy graders, but I can't say the same for the ones you will have in the future. I attended his lectures and after 5 I came to the conclusion that it was better to learn on my own. Shout out to Thomas Carr on YT.

Poor lecture material and at least 4 pages of homework per week (I write really small as well). I learned most of the material in high school but the hw still takes me over 3 hours due to the length of problems. Lectures aren't recorded unlike all of my other classes.
Lectures unclear, not recorded, first time teaching, class material used from other professors.
